epic.title: Sprint 0 — Foundations & setup
epic.summary: Everything the team needs in place before feature work begins: repositories and solution structure, CI/CD, cloud environments in Australia East, identity wiring for staff and clients, the Postgres + RLS data baseline, the API/web/app shells, a shared design system, observability, a security baseline, and the four de-risk spikes called out in the docs index. Nothing here ships clinical value on its own — it makes M1+ possible and safe.

stories.title: Monorepo & solution scaffolding
stories.context: A single, well-structured repo (or workspace) for the .NET API, Angular web, two Flutter apps and shared packages keeps versioning, CI and code-sharing sane from day one.
stories.i_want: a scaffolded repo with the API, web, app and shared-package folders, a clean .NET solution, an Angular workspace and a Flutter workspace wired together
stories.so_that: everyone builds against one consistent structure and shared code has an obvious home
acceptance: Repo layout documented in a top-level README (api/, web/, apps/, packages/, infra/, scripts/).
.NET solution builds; Angular workspace serves; both Flutter app shells run on a simulator.
Shared design-system and API-client packages exist as empty-but-buildable libraries.
Editorconfig, formatter and linter configs committed and enforced locally.

stories.title: CI/CD pipelines (build, test, deploy)
stories.context: Continuous build/test on every PR and automated deploys to environments prevent integration drift and make the compliance posture (tests, scans) enforceable.
stories.i_want: pipelines that build and test the API, web and apps on every PR and deploy to dev/staging on merge
stories.so_that: broken code is caught early and releases are repeatable
acceptance: PR pipeline runs build + unit/integration tests for API, web and apps and blocks merge on failure.
Merge to main deploys API + web to the dev environment automatically.
Mobile app builds produce installable artifacts for internal distribution.
Pipeline status is required for merge (branch protection ties in via S0-GOV).

stories.title: Cloud environments & infrastructure-as-code (AU East)
stories.context: All PII/PHI must resolve to Australia East (C21/ADR-0016). Defining dev/staging/prod as code makes residency, isolation and reproducibility provable.
stories.i_want: dev, staging and prod environments provisioned via IaC, all pinned to Australia East
stories.so_that: data residency is guaranteed and environments are reproducible
acceptance: IaC provisions compute, Postgres, blob storage and networking in Australia East for each environment.
A non-AU region for any PII/PHI resource fails the IaC policy check.
Environments are isolated (separate data stores, secrets, identities).
Tear-down/re-create of a non-prod environment is a single command.

stories.title: Postgres + EF Core baseline & migrations
stories.context: A migrations-first data layer with a base entity convention (tenant_id, audit columns) underpins every module.
stories.i_want: a Postgres database with EF Core, a migration pipeline and base entity/columns conventions
stories.so_that: every module adds schema consistently and migrations run automatically per environment
acceptance: EF Core connects to Postgres; migrations apply automatically on deploy.
Base entity convention includes tenant_id and created/updated audit columns.
A sample entity round-trips through repository + migration in tests.
Local dev uses a containerised Postgres seeded by S0-SEED.

stories.title: Row-level-security multi-tenancy baseline
stories.context: Tenant isolation is enforced in the database via RLS, with the API setting tenant context per request (ADR-0003). This is the single most important safety property of the data layer.
stories.i_want: Postgres RLS policies keyed on tenant_id, with EF Core setting the session tenant context on every request
stories.so_that: a query can never return another tenant's rows, even on a developer mistake
acceptance: RLS policies are applied to all tenant-scoped tables.
API middleware sets the session tenant from the authenticated principal on every request.
An integration test proves Tenant A cannot read Tenant B rows and a cross-tenant id returns not-found.
Bypassing tenant context (e.g. background jobs) uses an explicit, audited elevated path.

stories.title: Staff identity: Entra ID SSO + MFA wiring
stories.context: Staff sign in with the clinic's existing Microsoft 365 accounts via Entra ID with MFA (ADR-0004). This is the plumbing; the RBAC/scope rules are PRD-01.
stories.i_want: to sign in to the web and provider app with my Microsoft 365 account and MFA
stories.so_that: I use one secure corporate identity and no extra password
acceptance: Entra ID app registration configured; web + provider app complete SSO + MFA.
Tokens carry the tenant and a stable user id usable by the API.
Sign-out and token refresh work across web and app.
Sessions are scoped to a single tenant.

stories.title: Client identity: Entra External ID (social / email / OTP) wiring
stories.context: Clients create accounts with Google/Apple, email+password, or email/SMS OTP via Entra External ID (CIAM).
stories.i_want: to create an account and sign in with social login, email+password, or a one-time code
stories.so_that: I can access booking, intake and my records easily and securely
acceptance: Entra External ID configured for social, local (email+password) and OTP flows.
Client app and public web complete each of the three sign-in methods.
Client identities are tenant-scoped and distinct from staff identities.
Account recovery (password reset / OTP resend) works.

stories.title: .NET API skeleton: architecture, tenant context, error model
stories.context: A consistent API skeleton (clean/layered architecture, request pipeline, problem-details errors, pagination) means every feature module plugs in the same way.
stories.i_want: an API skeleton with auth middleware, tenant-context resolution, a standard error/response model and health checks
stories.so_that: feature modules are added consistently and cross-cutting concerns are solved once
acceptance: Auth middleware validates Entra tokens (staff + client) and resolves tenant context for RLS.
Standard problem-details error responses and consistent pagination/filtering conventions.
Health/readiness endpoints and structured request logging in place.
A vertical-slice sample endpoint demonstrates the full pattern end-to-end.

stories.title: Append-only audit infrastructure baseline
stories.context: C10/ADR-0010 require an immutable record of who read/changed clinical, medicines and PII data. Building the append-only AuditEvent plumbing now lets every later module just emit events.
stories.i_want: an append-only AuditEvent store and a simple API/interceptor for modules to record reads and writes
stories.so_that: auditability is a built-in default rather than a per-feature afterthought
acceptance: AuditEvent table is append-only (no update/delete path) and carries who/what/when/tenant.
A reusable interceptor/helper records create/update/read for annotated entities or endpoints.
Events are queryable and exportable (full register UI is PRD-01/PRD-08).
Tampering attempts (update/delete) are rejected at the data layer.

stories.title: OpenAPI contract + typed client generation
stories.context: Generating typed clients for Angular and Flutter from the API's OpenAPI spec keeps the three surfaces in lock-step and removes hand-written DTO drift (ADR-0006).
stories.i_want: the API to publish an OpenAPI document and CI to generate typed clients for web and Flutter
stories.so_that: front-ends always match the API contract
acceptance: API serves a versioned OpenAPI document.
Typed clients are generated for Angular and Flutter and published to the shared packages.
Client generation runs in CI and fails on a breaking contract change without a version bump.
The sample endpoint is consumed via the generated client in both web and app.

stories.title: Angular web shell: routing, auth guard, layout
stories.context: The admin/front-desk web app needs a shell — auth guard, role-aware nav, layout — before feature screens land.
stories.i_want: an Angular shell with the Entra auth guard, top-level routing, a role-aware navigation frame and the design-system theme applied
stories.so_that: feature screens drop into a consistent, authenticated layout
acceptance: Unauthenticated users are redirected to Entra sign-in; authenticated users land on a shell.
Navigation is driven by capabilities/role (placeholder until PRD-01 RBAC lands).
Design-system theme + tokens applied globally.
A sample feature route renders inside the shell using the generated API client.

stories.title: Design system / shared component library + tokens
stories.context: One design system (tokens, components) shared across web and the two Flutter apps delivers the 'fast and modern, thumb-first' UX the docs demand and avoids re-styling per surface.
stories.i_want: a shared set of design tokens and core components (buttons, inputs, banners, the blocked-action banner, chips, tables)
stories.so_that: all surfaces look consistent and the compliance UX patterns are reusable
acceptance: Tokens (colour, type, spacing) defined once and consumed by web + Flutter.
Core components include the cross-cutting patterns from UX §4 (blocked-action banner, consent/age chips, S4 guardrail tag, offline indicator).
Components are documented in a simple gallery/storybook.
Accessibility basics (contrast, focus, hit-target size) verified for core components.

stories.title: Flutter app shells (client + provider) + shared packages
stories.context: One Flutter codebase, two flavours (client, provider), sharing auth, the API client and the design system (ADR-0006).
stories.i_want: client and provider app shells with bottom-tab navigation, shared auth/API/design packages and secure token storage
stories.so_that: the two apps are built from one codebase with consistent plumbing
acceptance: Client app shell has the Home/Book/My care/Membership/Account tabs; provider app shell has Schedule/Patient/Medicines/Tasks tabs (empty screens).
Both apps sign in (client = External ID, provider = Entra) and call the sample endpoint.
Auth tokens stored in secure storage; design-system theme applied.
Builds are produced by CI for internal distribution.

stories.title: Secrets & configuration management
stories.context: Connection strings, provider keys (Square, SMS, Xero) and signing secrets must live in a managed vault, never in code or config files.
stories.i_want: a managed secret store (Key Vault) wired into each environment and a typed config story for the API and apps
stories.so_that: no secret is ever committed and rotation is possible
acceptance: API and pipelines read secrets from the vault per environment; none in source control.
A secret-scanning check fails the build on a committed secret.
Config is environment-specific and typed; missing required config fails fast at startup.
Rotating a secret requires no code change.

stories.title: Observability: logging, tracing, metrics, alerting
stories.context: You cannot run a compliance-critical platform blind. Centralised logs, distributed traces, metrics and alerting are needed from the first deploy.
stories.i_want: structured logging, distributed tracing, key metrics dashboards and alerting wired across API and apps
stories.so_that: we can diagnose issues and detect incidents (feeding the breach workflow) quickly
acceptance: Structured logs and traces flow to a central store with correlation ids across web/app/API.
Dashboards cover request rate, latency, errors and key resource health.
Alerts fire for error-rate and availability thresholds.
Audit/security-relevant signals are distinguishable to feed the breach workflow (PRD-01).

stories.title: Security baseline: encryption, headers, dependency & secret scanning
stories.context: Encryption in transit + at rest, least-privilege, secure headers and automated scanning establish the security posture the compliance docs assume (C10/C21).
stories.i_want: TLS everywhere, encryption at rest, hardened HTTP headers, least-privilege identities and dependency/secret/code scanning in CI
stories.so_that: the platform meets its baseline security obligations from day one
acceptance: All traffic is TLS; data stores are encrypted at rest.
Security headers (CSP, HSTS, etc.) applied to web; CORS locked down.
Dependency, secret and SAST scans run in CI and gate merges on high-severity findings.
Service identities follow least-privilege; no shared admin credentials.

stories.title: Test strategy & harness + quality gates
stories.context: A clinical platform needs a serious automated-test culture: unit, integration (incl. RLS/auth/audit invariants) and end-to-end, with coverage gates.
stories.i_want: test harnesses for API (unit + integration), web and apps, plus an e2e smoke suite and coverage gates in CI
stories.so_that: compliance invariants are protected by tests and regressions are caught automatically
acceptance: Unit + integration test projects exist for the API (integration tests run against a real Postgres with RLS).
Web and app unit/widget test setups in place; an e2e smoke test covers sign-in + the sample flow.
Coverage threshold enforced in CI.
A documented convention for writing 'compliance invariant' tests (gate must block) exists.

stories.title: Synthetic seed data & local dev environment
stories.context: Realistic synthetic data (clinic, staff with credentials, clients, services incl. S4/non-S4, stock) lets every module be developed and demoed without real PII. All data must stay synthetic (project rule).
stories.i_want: a one-command local environment seeded with synthetic tenant, staff, clients, catalogue and stock
stories.so_that: I can run and demo any module locally without touching real data
acceptance: A script spins up Postgres + API + web locally and seeds a synthetic tenant.
Seed covers staff roles/credentials, clients (incl. an under-18), services flagged S4/non-S4, and stock lots.
All seed data is clearly synthetic; no real client/staff/business info.
Seed is reproducible and used by integration/e2e tests.

stories.title: Repo governance: branch protection, PR & env protection
stories.context: Branch protection, required checks, PR templates and environment protection rules keep the main branch deployable and reviewable.
stories.i_want: protected main with required status checks and reviews, PR/issue templates, and protected prod deployments
stories.so_that: quality gates can't be bypassed and changes are traceable
acceptance: main requires passing checks + at least one review before merge.
PR and issue templates committed (PR template references the backlog item).
Production deploys require an approval gate.
CODEOWNERS routes reviews for sensitive areas (auth, medicines, compliance).

stories.title: Spike — Entra External ID ↔ Flutter ↔ .NET auth
stories.context: End-to-end auth across Entra (staff), Entra External ID (clients), Flutter and .NET is novel enough to de-risk before committing the auth wiring stories.
stories.i_want: a time-boxed spike proving staff + client sign-in from Flutter and web through to authorised .NET API calls
stories.so_that: the auth wiring stories proceed on a proven approach
acceptance: A throwaway prototype completes Entra (staff) and External ID (client) sign-in from Flutter and web.
An authorised call reaches a .NET endpoint with tenant + role claims usable for RLS/RBAC.
Findings, library choices and gotchas written up; risks/decisions captured (ADR if needed).
Outcome explicitly feeds AUTH-STAFF / AUTH-CLIENT.

stories.title: Spike — Postgres RLS with EF Core session context
stories.context: Setting the per-request tenant for RLS through EF Core's connection/session lifecycle has known sharp edges (pooling, background jobs). Prove it before building on it.
stories.i_want: a spike proving EF Core reliably sets the Postgres session tenant for RLS under connection pooling and async
stories.so_that: the RLS baseline is built on a verified pattern
acceptance: Prototype shows RLS isolation holds across pooled connections and async requests.
A safe elevated/bypass path for background jobs is demonstrated and audited.
Performance impact measured and acceptable.
Pattern documented and feeds SPRINT-0/RLS.

stories.title: Spike — Square AU card-on-file recurring autopay
stories.context: Membership autopay depends on Square AU supporting tokenised card-on-file recurring charges with dunning — flagged 🔬 in the docs. De-risk before committing PRD-06 memberships.
stories.i_want: a spike confirming Square AU can tokenise a card and run recurring charges with failure/dunning handling
stories.so_that: the membership autopay design is viable (or an alternative is chosen early)
acceptance: Prototype tokenises a test card and runs a scheduled recurring charge in Square AU sandbox.
Failed-charge / retry behaviour observed; dunning approach outlined.
PCI posture confirmed: no PAN stored, only provider tokens (ADR-0007).
Go/no-go + findings recorded; feeds PRD-06 memberships.

stories.title: Spike — Flutter injection-mapping canvas
stories.context: The injection-mapping canvas is the hero clinical screen and the highest app risk (tap-to-add + drag points on a facial diagram/photo, per-point metadata). Prove the approach before PRD-05.
stories.i_want: a spike of the Flutter canvas: tap-to-add and drag injection points over a facial diagram and a photo, each holding metadata
stories.so_that: PRD-05 charting builds on a proven, performant canvas
acceptance: Prototype supports tap-to-add, drag-to-move and per-point metadata over both a diagram and an image.
Performs smoothly on a mid-range device with many points.
Coordinate model survives image scaling/rotation and is persistable.
Approach documented; feeds PRD-05 mapping stories.

stories.title: Spike — offline queue & sync integrity (provider app)
stories.context: Treatment rooms drop Wi-Fi; the provider app must queue notes/photos encrypted and sync without loss, with server-side finalisation (ADR-0015/0010). De-risk before PRD-05/09.
stories.i_want: a spike proving an encrypted local queue for drafts/photos that syncs with no data loss and last-write-wins drafts
stories.so_that: the offline-tolerant charting design is viable
acceptance: Prototype queues drafts + a photo offline (encrypted) and syncs cleanly on reconnect with no loss.
Conflict handling (last-write-wins for drafts) demonstrated; finalisation is server-side.
Photos never persist on device beyond a transient sync cache (C14/ADR-0009).
Approach documented; feeds PRD-05 / PRD-09.

stories.title: Background jobs & scheduler infrastructure
stories.context: Many features are time-driven, not user-driven: reminders/recall sends, membership autopay + dunning retries, retention timers, temperature polling, expiry alerts, read-model projections. A reliable background worker + scheduler is needed before those features land.
stories.i_want: a background-job framework with scheduling, retries, idempotency and dead-lettering
stories.so_that: time-driven and async work runs reliably and observably
acceptance: A worker host runs scheduled + queued jobs with retry, back-off and dead-letter handling.
Jobs are tenant-aware and run under an audited elevated context (per RLS bypass path).
Job runs are observable (metrics/logs/alerts) via the Sprint 0 observability stack.
A sample recurring job (e.g. expiry scan) and a sample queued job are demonstrated.

stories.title: Media storage & signed-URL service
stories.context: Clinical photos and documents must be stored centrally in AU, served via short-lived signed URLs, and never persisted on personal devices (C14/ADR-0009). A shared media service underpins PRD-05 photos and PRD-09 capture.
stories.i_want: a media service for encrypted AU-resident blob storage with signed-URL upload/download
stories.so_that: photos/documents are stored and served securely and consistently
acceptance: Blob storage is AU-resident and encrypted at rest; access is via short-lived signed URLs only.
Upload + download flows are demonstrated from web and Flutter.
Media access is consent- and capability-checked and audited.
No public/unsigned access to media is possible.

stories.title: Domain-event / messaging backbone
stories.context: Reporting read-models, the follow-up job queue, notifications and cross-module reactions all consume domain events. A lightweight event backbone (outbox + dispatch) decouples modules.
stories.i_want: a domain-event mechanism (transactional outbox + dispatch) modules can publish to and subscribe from
stories.so_that: read-models, jobs and notifications react to changes without tight coupling
acceptance: Modules publish domain events transactionally (outbox) with at-least-once dispatch.
Subscribers (read-models, jobs, notifications) can consume events idempotently.
Events carry tenant context and are observable.
A sample event flows from a write to a read-model projection.

stories.title: Feature flags & per-tenant configuration
stories.context: The build is staged and many capabilities are configurable per tenant (cooling-off policy, S4 naming, telehealth path risk, modality availability, deferred features). A flag + config mechanism lets features ship dark and toggle per tenant.
stories.i_want: feature flags and a typed per-tenant configuration store
stories.so_that: we can ship features progressively and tailor behaviour per clinic
acceptance: Feature flags can be toggled globally and per tenant.
Per-tenant configuration (e.g. cooling-off N days, S4 naming, modality availability) is typed and validated.
Flag/config reads are cached and observable.
Changing a flag/config is audited.
